meta.certificationName: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300)
meta.title: MS-PBI-6: Efficient Models, Governed Delivery, and Interactive Analysis

questions.stem: A Power Query step should be executed by the source system whenever possible to improve refresh efficiency. Which concept describes this behavior?
questions.explanation: Query folding pushes supported transformations back to the source so less work is done in the local mashup engine.

questions.stem: Which model design is generally recommended for Power BI semantic models?

options.text: A star schema with fact and dimension tables
A single wide spreadsheet-style table for every solution
A many-to-many relationship between every table

questions.explanation: Star schema design supports clearer semantics and better usability and performance in Power BI than indiscriminate flat or many-to-many designs.

questions.stem: A report author wants users to navigate to a page filtered to the selected customer from another visual. Which feature is designed for that?
questions.explanation: Drillthrough sends filter context from one visual to a target page for focused analysis of the selected item.

questions.stem: A dataset refreshes from on-premises SQL Server into the Power BI service. Which component is typically required?
questions.explanation: The on-premises data gateway enables the Power BI service to access supported on-premises data sources securely for refresh.

questions.stem: Why is a measure usually preferred over a calculated column for an aggregation such as total sales?

options.text: A measure is evaluated at query time in filter context and does not store a value per row
A measure can only be used in DirectQuery models
A calculated column cannot reference numeric data

questions.explanation: Measures are calculated at query time and are often more storage efficient for aggregations than calculated columns, which store values row by row.

questions.stem: A transformation in Power Query breaks folding unexpectedly. What is the most likely consequence?

options.text: More processing may occur in the mashup engine instead of the source
The model automatically becomes Import plus DirectQuery

questions.explanation: When folding stops, subsequent steps may run outside the source system and can increase refresh cost and duration.

questions.stem: A team wants to distribute a curated collection of dashboards and reports to business users while managing audiences centrally. Which capabilities support that goal? (Select TWO)

options.text: Publish content through a Power BI app
Replace the model with CSV exports
Assign report themes as security boundaries

questions.explanation: Apps and app audiences are the supported way to package and distribute curated content to user groups. CSV exports and themes do not manage governed distribution.

questions.stem: A fact table has transaction dates, and several time intelligence measures must work correctly. What should the model include?

options.text: A proper date table marked as a date table

questions.explanation: Time intelligence works best with a dedicated, contiguous date table marked appropriately in the model.

questions.stem: A report page should show detailed context when a user hovers over a point in a chart without navigating away. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: Report page tooltips provide contextual detail on hover while preserving the user’s current location.

questions.stem: A team combines two tables with the same columns by stacking rows from one after the other. Which Power Query transformation is used?
questions.explanation: Append queries adds rows from one table to another with similar schema, while merge performs a join.

questions.stem: Which modeling choices usually improve Power BI performance and usability? (Select TWO)

options.text: Remove unused columns from imported tables
Prefer a clean star schema over arbitrary snowflaking where possible
Use bidirectional filtering on every relationship by default
Store high-cardinality text duplicates in every fact table

questions.explanation: Removing unused columns reduces model size, and a clean star schema improves both usability and performance. Blanket bidirectional filtering and duplicated text generally hurt models.

questions.stem: A model contains sensitive financial data and should carry data-classification metadata into supported Microsoft services. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: Sensitivity labels help classify and protect Power BI content and can integrate with broader Microsoft information protection features.

questions.stem: A report needs a button that changes visible visuals and preserved filter states to tell a guided story on one page. Which feature best supports this?
questions.explanation: Bookmarks can capture display state and filter context and can be connected to buttons for guided navigation.

questions.stem: A DAX author needs to modify filter context before evaluating an expression. Which function is central to that task?
questions.explanation: CALCULATE evaluates an expression in a modified filter context and is foundational to many DAX measures.

questions.stem: A source system contains repeated attribute columns like `Jan`, `Feb`, and `Mar`, but the analyst wants one `Month` column and one `Value` column. Which Power Query transformation is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Unpivot converts multiple attribute columns into row values, which is useful for normalization and analysis.

questions.stem: A semantic model in Import mode is too large to refresh quickly, but historical partitions rarely change. Which feature should be considered?
questions.explanation: Incremental refresh reduces refresh volume by refreshing recent partitions while keeping historical data intact.

questions.stem: A many-to-many relationship is necessary between dimensions through shared entities. What is usually the best way to keep the model understandable?

options.text: Use an appropriate bridge table pattern
Duplicate the fact table for each dimension
Disable all relationships and use bookmarks

questions.explanation: A bridge table is the usual pattern for resolving many-to-many business relationships while maintaining model clarity.

questions.stem: Which capabilities help users explore a report interactively without editing the semantic model? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Drillthrough and bookmarks change the analytical experience for end users. Gateways and labels address infrastructure or governance, not interactive report exploration.

questions.stem: A team wants to combine customer records from two tables by matching `CustomerID`. Which Power Query transformation should be used?
questions.explanation: Merge queries performs a join between tables using matching keys; append only stacks rows.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a Power BI workspace?

options.text: To collaborate on and manage content before distribution
To store on-premises gateway binaries only
To define the schema of a source database

questions.explanation: Workspaces are collaboration and management containers for datasets, reports, dashboards, and apps in the service.

questions.stem: A report author needs a year-over-year comparison measure. Why is the date table especially important?

options.text: Time intelligence functions rely on a proper date table and date context
It increases query folding in every source
It eliminates the need for relationships

questions.explanation: Time intelligence functions such as year-over-year comparisons depend on a valid date table and correct relationships.

questions.stem: A line chart should highlight unexpected spikes and drops automatically in supported scenarios. Which feature can help?
questions.explanation: Power BI anomaly detection can surface unusual points in supported line charts to aid analysis.

questions.stem: A company must secure a report so regional managers only see rows for their region in the service. Which actions are appropriate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Test the result with View as or equivalent role testing
Replace measures with calculated columns only

questions.explanation: RLS roles and validation/testing are the right practices for restricting row visibility by user or group. The other choices do not implement the security requirement.

questions.stem: A model imports a huge detail table and a much smaller summary table to speed up frequent queries. Which feature is explicitly designed for this pattern?
questions.explanation: Aggregations let Power BI answer compatible queries from summarized tables while preserving drill-down to detail when needed.

questions.stem: A report must connect to another Power BI semantic model and also a few extra local tables. Which modeling capability enables this?
questions.explanation: Composite models allow combining DirectQuery to semantic models or external sources with other tables in one model.

questions.stem: Which visual behavior occurs when selecting a data point in one visual causes related points in another visual to be emphasized?
questions.explanation: Cross-highlighting is the interactive behavior where related data remains visible but emphasized across visuals.

questions.stem: Business users complain that slicer changes fire too many queries. Which feature can reduce unnecessary query traffic?

options.text: Query reduction options such as an Apply button for slicers

questions.explanation: Query reduction features can delay queries until users finish slicer changes, reducing noisy traffic.

questions.stem: Which steps are often used when shaping data in Power Query? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Changing data types and pivoting or unpivoting are common shaping tasks in Power Query. Workspace roles and app audiences belong to service administration and distribution.

questions.stem: What is the main risk of enabling bidirectional filtering broadly without a clear reason?

options.text: It can create ambiguous filter paths and confusing behavior
It prevents use of date tables

questions.explanation: Bidirectional filtering can introduce ambiguity and unexpected propagation, so it should be used deliberately.

questions.stem: A team wants a report page that supports self-service exploration with alternate dimensions and measures chosen by the consumer. Which Power BI feature is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Field parameters let report consumers switch dimensions or measures in supported visuals for flexible analysis.

questions.stem: A workspace owner wants a reusable governed package of content for different groups, each with slightly different visibility. What should they publish?

options.text: A Power BI app with audiences
A CSV file for each team

questions.explanation: Apps with audiences are the supported content packaging and distribution mechanism for different user groups.

questions.stem: Which statements about DAX evaluation context are true? (Select TWO)

options.text: Measures are evaluated in filter context
CALCULATE can transition or modify context for an expression
Relationships are optional for filter propagation

questions.explanation: DAX measures are evaluated in filter context, and CALCULATE modifies that context. Calculated columns do store results, and relationships matter for propagation.

questions.stem: An analyst sees that the first few applied steps fold, but a custom transformation step does not. What should the analyst consider first?

options.text: Whether the custom step can be rewritten into a foldable transformation
Whether bookmarks can capture the transformation
Whether the workspace needs Premium capacity
Whether all measures should be hidden

questions.explanation: When folding breaks, the first optimization is often to rewrite the non-folding step into a transformation the source can support.

questions.stem: A consumer should move from a summary page to a details page while carrying the selected product category. Which feature provides the most natural experience?
questions.explanation: Drillthrough passes current filter context to a dedicated details page for targeted investigation.

questions.stem: A dataset uses Import mode and must refresh from multiple on-premises sources with high availability. What should the team plan for?

options.text: A properly configured gateway deployment and refresh management process

questions.explanation: Reliable refresh from on-premises sources depends on gateway architecture and operational management, not report interactivity features.

questions.stem: Which modeling technique most directly reduces model size when a verbose text field is not needed for analytics?

options.text: Remove the column from the model
Create a bookmark for the field
Move it to a tooltip page

questions.explanation: Removing unused columns is a straightforward model-size optimization and can improve refresh and query performance.

questions.stem: A report author wants to ensure numeric comparisons behave correctly after import from CSV. What should be verified early in Power Query?
questions.explanation: Correct data types are fundamental for accurate calculations, sorting, and aggregations.

questions.stem: Which features are designed to improve storytelling and user-guided analysis in reports? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Bookmarks guide navigation and state, while tooltips add rich contextual detail during exploration. Credentials and role membership are administrative controls.

questions.stem: A security reviewer wants to know whether row-level security is defined inside the report visual layer or the semantic model. Which answer is correct?

options.text: RLS is defined on the model by roles and filter expressions
RLS exists only in the app navigation pane

questions.explanation: RLS is implemented in the semantic model by defining roles and their filter logic.

questions.stem: A team has many repetitive time-intelligence measures and wants a reusable semantic-model technique to reduce duplication. Which feature is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Calculation groups can centralize reusable calculation logic such as time-intelligence patterns and reduce measure proliferation.
